annotations.label: Riemann surface (lead)

anchor.snippet: a Riemann surface is a connected one-dimensional complex manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has general complex manifolds via IsManifold over a ℂ-model, but no defined object 'Riemann surface' with the connected one-complex-dimensional restriction.

annotations.label: Graphs of multivalued functions

anchor.snippet: Examples of Riemann surfaces include graphs of multivalued functions

annotations.note: No formalization of multivalued-function graphs as Riemann surfaces in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real manifold admits complex structure iff orientable and metrizable

anchor.snippet: a two-dimensional real manifold can be turned into a Riemann surface

annotations.note: No result relating real surface orientability/metrizability to existence of a complex structure.

annotations.label: Surfaces admitting / not admitting complex structures

anchor.snippet: the sphere and torus admit complex structures but the Möbius strip

annotations.note: No Mathlib facts on which of S², T², Möbius strip, Klein bottle, ℝP² admit a complex (Riemann surface) structure.

annotations.label: Compact Riemann surface is a complex algebraic curve

anchor.snippet: Every compact Riemann surface is a complex algebraic curve by Chow's theorem

annotations.note: Chow's theorem and the analytic-to-algebraic correspondence for compact Riemann surfaces are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemann surface as complex manifold of dimension one

anchor.snippet: is a connected complex manifold of complex dimension one

annotations.note: Complex manifolds are modeled by IsManifold over a ℂ-model-with-corners, but the one-complex-dimensional 'Riemann surface' is not a defined notion.

annotations.label: Riemann surface via conformal structure

anchor.snippet: is a (connected) oriented manifold of (real) dimension two

annotations.note: Mathlib has a conformalGroupoid but no definition of a Riemann surface as an oriented 2-manifold with a conformal structure.

annotations.label: Complex structure induces conformal structure

anchor.snippet: A complex structure gives rise to a conformal structure

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a holomorphic map with nonvanishing derivative is conformal at a point, but not the manifold-level statement that a complex structure induces a conformal structure.

annotations.label: Complex plane

anchor.snippet: is the most basic Riemann surface

annotations.note: ℂ carries the model-space complex manifold structure via instIsManifoldModelSpace, though it is not labeled a Riemann surface.

annotations.label: Open subset of complex plane

anchor.snippet: Every nonempty connected open subset of the complex plane

annotations.note: Open subsets of a manifold (hence of ℂ) inherit a manifold structure via Opens.instIsManifoldSubtypeMem, but not framed as Riemann surfaces.

annotations.label: Open subset of a Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: every non-empty open subset of a Riemann surface is a Riemann surface

annotations.note: Mathlib's Opens.instIsManifoldSubtypeMem gives that any open subset of a manifold is again a manifold, generalizing the Riemann-surface case.

annotations.label: Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: has a unique Riemann surface structure, called the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib has the one-point compactification/projective line topologically but no complex-manifold (Riemann sphere) structure on it.

annotations.label: Riemann sphere as projective line

anchor.snippet: The Riemann sphere has another description, as the projective line

annotations.note: Mathlib has the projective line ℙ¹(ℂ) as a set via Projectivization, but does not equip it with the Riemann sphere complex-manifold structure.

annotations.label: Torus / elliptic curves

anchor.snippet: has many different Riemann surface structures

annotations.note: No complex-torus quotient ℂ/Λ is constructed as a Riemann surface in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-compact surfaces from analytic continuation

anchor.snippet: Important examples of non-compact Riemann surfaces are provided by analytic continuation

annotations.note: Analytic-continuation construction of Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vanishing locus of a polynomial

anchor.snippet: is any complex polynomial in two variables

annotations.note: The smooth vanishing locus of a complex polynomial as a Riemann surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Every elliptic curve is an algebraic curve

anchor.snippet: Every elliptic curve is an algebraic curve

annotations.note: Mathlib defines elliptic curves algebraically as Weierstrass curves (IsElliptic), but does not formalize the analytic-curve to algebraic-curve identification.

annotations.label: Hyperelliptic surfaces of higher genus

anchor.snippet: surfaces have Riemann surface structures, as ( compactifications of) hyperelliptic surfaces

annotations.note: Hyperelliptic Riemann surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Holomorphic map between Riemann surfaces

anchor.snippet: is called holomorphic if for every chart

annotations.note: Holomorphic maps between complex manifolds are captured by MDifferentiable over ℂ, but not specialized/named for Riemann surfaces.

annotations.label: Composition of holomorphic maps is holomorphic

anchor.snippet: The composition of two holomorphic maps is holomorphic

annotations.note: MDifferentiable.comp proves composition of differentiable maps between manifolds (hence holomorphic maps over ℂ) is again differentiable.

annotations.label: Biholomorphic / conformally equivalent surfaces

anchor.snippet: are called biholomorphic (or conformally equivalent

annotations.note: Mathlib has Diffeomorph (differentiable equivalences of manifolds), which over ℂ gives biholomorphisms, but no dedicated biholomorphism/conformal-equivalence definition.

annotations.label: Riemann surfaces are orientable

anchor.snippet: Each Riemann surface, being a complex manifold, is orientable as a real manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has linear-algebra orientations but no notion of orientability of a manifold, so this statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jacobian determinant of complex multiplication is |a|²

anchor.snippet: the real determinant of multiplication by a complex number

annotations.note: Could not locate a Mathlib lemma stating the real determinant of multiplication by a complex scalar equals |a|² (the cited Complex.det_conformalLinearMap does not exist).

annotations.label: Non-compact surfaces admit non-constant holomorphic functions

anchor.snippet: Every non-compact Riemann surface admits non-constant holomorphic functions

annotations.note: Existence of non-constant holomorphic functions on non-compact Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-compact Riemann surface is a Stein manifold

anchor.snippet: every non-compact Riemann surface is a Stein manifold

annotations.note: Stein manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Holomorphic functions on compact surfaces are constant

anchor.snippet: is constant due to the maximum principle

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a holomorphic function on a compact connected complex manifold is constant, of which a compact Riemann surface is a special case.

annotations.label: Existence of non-constant meromorphic functions

anchor.snippet: there always exist non-constant meromorphic functions

annotations.note: Existence of non-constant meromorphic functions on a compact Riemann surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Function field is a finite extension

anchor.snippet: any two meromorphic functions are algebraically dependent

annotations.note: The function field of a compact Riemann surface and its transcendence-degree-one property are not formalized.

annotations.label: Compact Riemann surfaces are algebraic curves

anchor.snippet: All compact Riemann surfaces are algebraic curves

annotations.note: The theorem that compact Riemann surfaces are algebraic curves is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kodaira embedding for compact Riemann surfaces

anchor.snippet: This follows from the Kodaira embedding theorem

annotations.note: The Kodaira embedding theorem and positive line bundles on complex curves are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compact Riemann surface is a projective variety

anchor.snippet: any compact Riemann surface is a projective variety

annotations.note: No formalization of the projective-variety structure of a compact Riemann surface.

annotations.label: Embedding into complex projective 3-space

anchor.snippet: every compact Riemann surface can be embedded into complex projective 3-space

annotations.note: Embedding of compact Riemann surfaces into ℂP³ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Torus, Weierstrass function and elliptic curve

anchor.snippet: As an example, consider the torus

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Weierstrass ℘-function on a period lattice and proves ℘'² = 4℘³ − g₂℘ − g₃, but does not assemble the torus into an elliptic-curve Riemann surface.

annotations.label: Three-fold geometric classification

anchor.snippet: The set of all Riemann surfaces can be divided into three subsets

annotations.note: The elliptic/parabolic/hyperbolic geometric classification of Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Koebe uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: every simply connected Riemann surface is conformally equivalent to one of the following

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic by universal cover

anchor.snippet: A Riemann surface is elliptic, parabolic or hyperbolic according to whether its universal cover

annotations.note: Classification of Riemann surfaces by universal cover type is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann sphere is the only elliptic surface

anchor.snippet: is the only example, as there is no group acting on it

annotations.note: The fact that the Riemann sphere is the unique elliptic Riemann surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of parabolic surfaces

anchor.snippet: is a Riemann surface whose universal cover is isomorphic to the complex plane

annotations.note: Classification of parabolic Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Three topological types: plane, cylinder, torus

anchor.snippet: Topologically there are only three types: the plane, the cylinder and the torus

annotations.note: The topological trichotomy of parabolic surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Teichmüller space and moduli (parabolic)

anchor.snippet: gives the Teichmüller space of "marked" Riemann surfaces

annotations.note: Teichmüller space, the mapping class group action, and the resulting modular curve are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic surface as quotient by Fuchsian group

anchor.snippet: is a hyperbolic Riemann surface, that is isomorphic to a quotient of the upper half-plane by a Fuchsian group

annotations.note: Fuchsian groups and hyperbolic surfaces as upper-half-plane quotients are not formalized (Mathlib has the upper half-plane and Möbius action but not this construction).

annotations.label: Topological types of hyperbolic surfaces

anchor.snippet: can be any orientable surface save the torus and sphere

annotations.note: The classification of topological types of hyperbolic surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compact hyperbolic surface classified by genus

anchor.snippet: is compact. Then its topological type is described by its genus

annotations.note: Genus-based classification of compact hyperbolic surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Constraints on maps between geometric types

anchor.snippet: maps from hyperbolic to parabolic to elliptic are easy

annotations.note: Constraints on holomorphic maps between the geometric types are not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville and Little Picard constancy

anchor.snippet: any holomorphic map from the sphere to the plane is constant

annotations.note: Liouville's theorem (bounded entire functions are constant) is formalized, but Little Picard's theorem and the manifold framing are not.

annotations.label: Type of punctured spheres

anchor.snippet: With no punctures, it is the Riemann sphere, which is elliptic

annotations.note: Geometric type of punctured spheres is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compact surfaces map only to lower genus

anchor.snippet: compact Riemann surfaces can map to surfaces of lower genus

annotations.note: The genus constraint on maps between compact Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-constant maps are ramified coverings

anchor.snippet: non-constant maps are ramified covering maps

annotations.note: Ramified covering structure of non-constant holomorphic maps is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann–Hurwitz formula

anchor.snippet: constrained by the Riemann–Hurwitz formula

annotations.note: The Riemann–Hurwitz formula relating Euler characteristics of a ramified cover is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic surfaces cover the sphere

anchor.snippet: hyperbolic Riemann surfaces are ramified covering spaces of the sphere

annotations.note: This covering-space statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isometry group reflects geometry

anchor.snippet: The isometry group of a uniformized Riemann surface

annotations.note: Isometry groups of uniformized Riemann surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hurwitz's automorphisms theorem bound

anchor.snippet: the isometry group is finite, and has order at most

annotations.note: Hurwitz's 84(g−1) automorphism bound is not formalized.

annotations.label: Every finite group is an isometry group

anchor.snippet: every finite group can be realized as the full group of isometries of some Riemann surface

annotations.note: Realization of every finite group as a Riemann surface isometry group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Genus 2 Bolza surface

anchor.snippet: For genus 2 the order is maximized by the Bolza surface

annotations.note: The Bolza surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Genus 3 Klein quartic

anchor.snippet: For genus 3 the order is maximized by the Klein quartic

annotations.note: The Klein quartic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simple group of order 168 is second-smallest non-abelian simple

anchor.snippet: the unique simple group of order 168, which is the second-smallest non-abelian simple group

annotations.note: Uniqueness/identification of PSL(2,7) ≅ GL(3,2) as the simple group of order 168 is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus 4 Bring's surface

anchor.snippet: For genus 4, Bring's surface is a highly symmetric surface

annotations.note: Bring's surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Genus 7 Macbeath surface

anchor.snippet: For genus 7 the order is maximized by the Macbeath surface

annotations.note: The Macbeath surface is not formalized.

annotations.label: Function-theoretic parabolic/hyperbolic

anchor.snippet: a Riemann surface is called parabolic if there are no non-constant negative subharmonic functions

annotations.note: Subharmonic functions are not defined in Mathlib, so the function-theoretic classification is not formalized.

annotations.label: Subclasses of hyperbolic surfaces

anchor.snippet: This class of hyperbolic surfaces is further subdivided into subclasses

annotations.note: Subclasses (O_G, O_HP, etc.) of hyperbolic surfaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Disk: parabolic vs hyperbolic classifications

anchor.snippet: is parabolic in the function-theoretic classification but it is hyperbolic in the geometric classification

annotations.note: The differing classifications of the unit disk are not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann–Roch theorem (listed)

anchor.snippet: Riemann–Roch theorem

annotations.note: The Riemann–Roch theorem for compact Riemann surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Identity theorem for Riemann surfaces (listed)

anchor.snippet: Identity theorem for Riemann surfaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has the identity theorem for analytic functions on a connected open subset, but not stated at the level of Riemann surfaces.
